This advisory has been withdrawn because it is a duplicate of GHSA-9pq7-mfwh-xx2j. This link is maintained to preserve external references.

### Original Description
phpMyFAQ before 4.1.2 contains an improper restriction of excessive authentication attempts vulnerability in the /admin/check endpoint, which accepts arbitrary user-id parameters without session binding or rate limiting. Unauthenticated attackers can brute-force any user's six-digit TOTP code by submitting POST requests with sequential token values, bypassing two-factor authentication to gain full administrative access.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-6626-79jh-5ccr", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-09T00:00:12Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6626-79jh-5ccr", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-6626-79jh-5ccr"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-15T19:17:01Z", "published_at": "2026-05-15T21:31:32Z", "references": ["https://github.com/thorsten/phpMyFAQ/security/advisories/GHSA-9pq7-mfwh-xx2j", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-45010", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/phpmyfaq-unauthenticated-two-factor-authentication-brute-force-via-admin-check-endpoint", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6626-79jh-5ccr"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "", "summary": "Duplicate Advisory: phpMyFAQ enables unauthenticated 2FA brute-force attack via /admin/check acceptance of arbitrary user-id", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-09T00:00:13Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-6626-79jh-5ccr", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "4.1.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "thorsten/phpmyfaq"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 4.1.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "4.1.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "phpmyfaq/phpmyfaq"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 4.1.2"}], "withdrawn_at": "2026-06-09T00:00:12Z"}

title: Duplicate Advisory: phpMyFAQ enables unauthenticated 2FA brute-force attack via /admin/check acceptance of arbitrary user-id
